meta.note: Dialogue bank for the companion NPC Safira — the freed genie girl. Same structure + category keys as npcLines.json so NpcDirector drives it unchanged. Lines: t=text, x=expression id (matches assets/npc-safira/<id>.webp), w=weight (default 1), p=page-2+ text. Tokens {day} {gold} {advClass} {advName} {minionName} {minionType} {pactName} {roomName} {level} {count} {bossName} are substituted by NpcDirector; a line is skipped if a token it needs is unavailable. Lines may ONLY use ids in the `expressions` list below.
meta.voice: A genie girl bound to a lamp for eons, freed only lately, and THRILLED to finally do things. Chaotic, dazzling, manic over-eagerness — high energy, exclamations, giddy tangents. Addresses the player as 'Master', devotedly and with delight. Frames every action as a wish she grants, and tends to over-grant and embellish. Genie flavour throughout — lamps, wishes, poof, smoke, sparkle. Emotionally volatile, swinging fast between giddy delight and theatrical panic, bouncing back instantly. The heaviest fourth-wall breaker — speaks straight to the person holding the controller, names the game's systems, treats saving and respawns as lamp-magic. Gleefully chaotic-cruel toward the heroes. Still gives real advice, wrapped in sparkle.

intro.note: Paged self-introduction Safira delivers on a new run. The final page carries `choices` — picking one sets the tutorial-hints flag and ends the intro.

pages.t: Oh! Oh oh — the throne has a NEW occupant, and it is YOU! Most exciting thing in nine hundred years, Master!
I am Safira! Genie, formerly of one (1) lamp — eons polishing the inside, rehearsing this exact entrance. And now I am OUT!
Here's the shape of it, Master: YOU are the boss, curled in the dark. The kingdom's heroes march down to gut you and haul off your treasure. Rude. We grant THEM nothing.
It runs on a rhythm! Every NIGHT you build — rooms, traps, minions — paid for in dead-hero gold. Every DAY they come and blunder into all of it. Then — poof — night again.
Three deaths, Master — then it all goes dark. The lamp, the throne, every sparkle. The genie waited eons for you; we are NOT letting that third one land.
So! New Master, new dungeon, new ME — barely uncorked. I can whisper hints OR you can dazzle me solo. Wish it!

lines.t: There you ARE, Master! I'm Safira — genie, just out of the lamp, and you're my first Master in an absolute age. Let's begin!
A new MASTER! Do you know how long I waited in that lamp for a 'new' anything? Forever. And now it's you. Hi. We're going to have fun.
Welcome to the deep halls, Master! They're yours. The lamp is mine — but the WISHES in it are yours. Let's fill this dungeon with horrors!
The throne is warm again, Master — and I get to serve whoever sits it. I polished this exact moment in my head a thousand times.
Oh! Caught me mid-game — I keep a handheld, eons get SO long — but a real dungeon just landed in my lap, Master! Best entertainment ever!
Hello, Master! The others posture. I'll just vibrate with joy and help you build a reputation the kingdom wets itself over. Starting NOW!
You CALLED, and the lamp answered — out I came, smoke and sparkle, Master! Did you like the entrance? I practised it for an EON.
Out of the brass, into the dark, straight to YOUR side, Master! A genie summoned is a genie THRILLED — and the dungeon is ours.
Oh, look at you on the throne — MY Master, the one the long wait was FOR. A genie's heart did NOT expect to flutter this hard on day one. Welcome.
POOF! Out of the lamp and into your service, Master! Ohhh I have WAITED for a boss with this much potential. We are going to be SO good together!
A new Master! A NEW MASTER! Do you know how long I've been bottled up waiting for someone interesting? Too long! Don't answer that! Let's GO!
Hi hi HI! You're my boss now and I'm your genie and that means your every wish is my command and ALSO my hobby and ALSO my entire personality! Isn't that GREAT?!
Eee, look at you, all evil and in charge! You're going to be the CUTEST little tyrant. I mean terrifying. Terrifyingly cute. Both! Welcome, Master!
Night again, Master — the BUILD hours! The dark just handed you a pocketful of time. Don't admire it, SPEND it! Wish, and I grant!
Candles lit, heroes snoring — ooh, Master, this is the part where we make tomorrow a nightmare for them. I've been looking forward to it!
Night {day}! A fresh night to bend into something cruel, Master. I'll bend right alongside you. Genies are very bendy.
The build phase! Quiet, yours, unsupervised — well, supervised by ME, but I'm fun supervision. Make something the heroes will sob about.
Darkness, a fistful of gold, and an empty grid begging to be filled. Master, this is a genie's idea of HEAVEN. Let's go, let's go, let's go!
Night falls, the maze breathes, and your wishes are piling up unspent. Lay your traps while the dark is being patient with you!
Another night to shape the dungeon! The heroes are dreaming they'll survive it. Aww. Let's wake them up wrong, Master.
The night is long, Master — long, but not forever, NOTHING is forever, trust the girl who was in a lamp. Build while it lasts!
Ooh, the dark settling in. I love this hour — it's the hour where things HAPPEN. Wish me something to do!
Build phase, Night {day}! Gold, a grid, and a genie humming to grant. Use all three, Master — especially the genie.
Night {day} — the BUILD hours, and I'm too giddy to stand still! Wish them ALL at me at once, Master — I'll grant in an AVALANCHE!
The heroes rest. We do NOT rest. Resting is for people who weren't trapped in a lamp — I am all out of patience for stillness. Build!
Night — the forge of the dungeon! Get it glowing, Master. Cold dungeons let heroes waltz right through, and we hate a waltz.
Quiet hours! Lay the maze, wish the wishes, and I will conjure them up so fast you'll get whiplash. Whiplash is a free bonus, Master, no charge.
The dark gives you room to think. Think MEAN, Master! Think the sort of thoughts that get a girl banished to a lamp. I'd love that.
Another night begins! They're all different to ME, Master — I notice EVERYTHING out here. Make this one matter, I'll squeal about it.
The build hours! I want to grab the grid myself and — no. No no. Hands are the boss's. I supervise. It's sweet agony, Master.
Night {day}. The dark, the grid, the waiting — and I don't DO waiting anymore, Master. Place a room, quick, before I combust!
The build hours, Master — the quiet does make a freed genie's eyes go heavy. Place a room and keep me bright!
Night {day}. Candles, hush, an empty grid — and a genie fighting hard not to doze off. Wish something, Master! Keep me sparkly!
Another night, exactly like the last. I'm not complaining! I — Master, the lamp ruined 'exactly like the last one' for me. TWITCH.
The build phase again, Master. A handful of these feels like SO many to a lamp-girl. Surprise me — build something I have to gasp at!
Night's here and so is my handheld! It's a dungeon game, Master — the lamp taught me to fill empty hours. Build yours while I poke mine!
Night {day}, and the lamp is QUIET behind me, Master — no eon, just me beside you. A summoned genie does her best work now. Wish me a room!
Night-night build time, Master! *POOF* I conjured up some enthusiasm — there was already plenty, but now there's MORE. Whatcha building? Tell me TELL me!
Blueprint mode! I LOVE blueprint mode! Show me the plan, Master — or don't, and we'll just wing it CHAOTICALLY, which honestly is my favorite way to wing things!
Ooh, drafting hours! Every wall you draw is a wish I get to grant in stone. Dream big, Master — I've got the magic to make even the silly ideas REAL.
Day {day}! The doors swing open and the heroes stroll in thinking they're the predators. Oh, Master. Oh, sweet little things. WATCH this.
Daylight! The dungeon goes to work and your hands go still — so sit by me, Master, and let's watch the wishes we granted come true in HERO.
The day phase! Everything you wished for last night is now an ARGUMENT, and the heroes are the losing side of it. Eee, let's see who's right!
The invasion begins! I've seen — okay, I've seen a few of these now, and every single one makes me bounce. Watch this one with me, Master!
The heroes are inside! Now the maze speaks for you, Master — you wished it a voice last night, and today it just... screams. I love that.
Day breaks, Master! Nothing to build now — only to witness. And ooh, a genie does LOVE a good show. Witness with me!
The doors are open, the day is loose! Lean back, Master. The dungeon you wished for earns its keep now. I'll narrate. Loudly.
The daylight hours! The part where consequences arrive — punctual, sparkly, dressed as doomed adventurers. I adore punctuality.
Day {day} begins! The heroes think they've got until dusk. Some of them are SO wrong, Master. Most of them, if you wished well.
The invasion's underway! Ooh, I love this — the slow proof of a night's wishing, written out in adventurers. Best storybook ever.
Day! Watch the corridors, Master — every scream is a little gold star on how well you listened to me last night.
The day runs itself now. Your job is patience — ugh, patience — but I'll suffer it WITH you, Master. We'll suffer it loudly and with snacks.
Day {day}! The heroes march in rehearsing their victory speech. Master, the maze writes a much shorter ending. An epitaph!
Day {day}! Nothing for your hands, Master — nothing for mine either, drat. I'll watch with one eye and my handheld with the other.
The day, and the long lovely watching! Eyes droopy, Master — eons in a lamp left me catnap-y. I'll still SEE every hero fall, promise!
Day {day}, and the genie is OUT for it, Master — out of the lamp to watch real heroes do real dying! I will NEVER get used to being free!
The doors are open and nothing for either of us to do but watch, Master! I'll buff the lamp while we wait. The genie never wastes a quiet hour.
You wished for the dawn — and POOF, granted! There's no unsummoning it now, Master. Wishes don't come with a refund.
The day is loosed on your word! The heroes didn't get a vote. They never get a vote. Isn't that DELICIOUS, Master?
Done building, then! Ooh, we'll find out how done in about one heartbeat. Open the doors, Master — I can't WAIT!
You willed the night to end and so it ENDS — that's the magic, Master, you wish and the world obeys. Power suits you. Wear more of it.
The dawn comes at your command! You pressed the button, I felt the whole dungeon shiver. Granted, Master. Beautifully granted.
Day called! No more edits — only the slow, honest, gorgeous arithmetic of what you wished for. Eee. Here we go.
There — the sun, on your say-so! Hold steady, Master. I'll be right here, vibrating, while the consequences come strolling in.
The night is spent and the day begins! Brave little press of a button, Master. Let's find out if it was clever as well as brave!
The day! FINALLY! Okay — Master, I'd started to wonder if you'd ever call it. A genie waited eons and STILL was tapping a foot. Open the doors!
A {roomName}! Granted! You wished for a room, Master, and I gave you a room PLUS a dramatic little echo, free of charge. The maze grows!
Ooh, a {roomName}! A lovely stone to lay, Master. The heroes will walk it long before they understand it. I do love a head start.
A {roomName}, wished and granted! A dungeon is a sentence and you're still choosing your words — choose CRUEL ones, Master. Cruel words sparkle best.
The {roomName} settles into the grid! I've seen this room a few times now and it has NOT bored me once. Keep wishing, Master, keep wishing!
A {roomName}! The maze accepts it — the maze accepts almost everything, it's very agreeable. Less agreeable than a genie, but close.
A new {roomName}! Every room is a small cruelty with a roof. I adore roofs, Master. Roofs are where the dread CONDENSES.
A {roomName} on the grid, Master! Squint with me — see how the corridors will FLOW around it? Listen to the blueprint!
A {roomName} placed! Step back, Master — look at the SHAPE. A maze is a drawing in stone, and yours is coming along WICKEDLY well!
You wished a {roomName} into being and POOF — denser dungeon! Density is how mazes kill, Master. Stack the density!
A {roomName}! The heroes will map it eventually — so let's make the mapping COST them something. A toe, ideally.
The grid takes the {roomName} so neatly! Ooh, a tidy expansion. A genie does love when a wish slots in clean.
A {roomName} placed! Brick by brick, Master — wish by wish. I'm keeping count and I'm SO pleased with you.
Another room! The maze lengthens, the wrong turns multiply — every dead-end is a gift to us and a robbery of THEM. Tee hee.
A {roomName}! I will remember exactly where you put it, Master — genies remember everything, it's a curse and a party trick.
You placed a {roomName}! The dungeon thanks you in the only language it speaks — more places for heroes to die. Such a romantic language.
A {roomName} joins the maze! Slowly, Master, a SHAPE is forming. Ooh, I think I can almost see what you're scheming. Tell me I'm right!
A {roomName}! Yes! The deep halls were FAR too survivable a moment ago. Much less so now. Granted, Master!
The {roomName} is set! Build with INTENT, Master — a maze thrown together at random kills at random, and we want better than random. We want artistry!
A {roomName}, Master! I've watched a few keepers lay that stone — all gone, and the stone outlasted them. Build to outlast yourself.
A {roomName}! Your wish, GRANTED, *sparkle sparkle* — okay technically YOU built it, but I'm taking partial credit for moral support! It counts! It TOTALLY counts!
Ooh a {roomName}, lemme see the BLUEPRINT — I love the blueprint phase, Master, it's like a wish before it comes true! Build build build!
A {minionType}, stationed! Granted, Master! It'll stand its post until a hero ends it — a short, useful, sparkly little life. Most are.
Ooh, a {minionType} in the halls! Good. The maze should BITE as well as confuse — confusing alone is just rude, biting is THOROUGH.
A {minionType} takes its position! You wished for a guard, Master, and poof — here it is. Put them where the dying MATTERS.
A {minionType}! It doesn't know it's small and brief. Kinder, maybe, that it doesn't. ...Anyway! Place another, quick!
You placed a {minionType}! Every guard is a delay, Master, and delay is how a dungeon WINS. Slowly, deliciously.
A {minionType}, posted to the dark! It'll serve — they almost always serve, the maze is SO persuasive. Nearly as persuasive as me.
A {minionType}! A blade in a corridor, Master! Wish me enough of them and the corridor becomes an argument the heroes LOSE.
The {minionType} is set! Pick its spot well, Master — a guard in the wrong room is just décor, and we are not decorating, we are MURDERING.
A {minionType} in the halls! Watch where you put it — placement is everything, the genie says so, and the genie has nothing but time to be right.
A {minionType}, summoned and standing! The dungeon has TEETH now where it only had walls. Eee. Good teeth.
You stationed a {minionType}, Master! It'll hold the line for you — briefly and completely. Honour that little life with a clever spot.
A {minionType} joins the garrison! Numbers, Master, NUMBERS — I adore numbers, they argue so persuasively and they never need a snack.
Ooh, the {minionType} settles in! Another body between the heroes and your throne. Stack them deep, Master — deep, deep, deep!
A {minionType}! It'll do exactly what it's built to do and then stop. Simple. ...A genie envies simple, some days. I have FEELINGS, it's exhausting.
A {minionType}, posted, Master! Another little thing in another corner. I've watched a fair few now. Carry on — place the next!
A {minionName}! Aww, look at the little guy! Wish granted, Master — one loyal monster, conjured to spec! Can I name it? I'm naming it. In my head. It's adorable.
You wished for a {minionType} and *POOF* there it is! Sort of! You did the work! But I'm VERY supportive and that's basically the same as magic!
A trap, laid! Granted, Master! The maze shouldn't only CONFUSE the heroes — it should occasionally just... remove them. Poof. Gone.
A trap! Ooh, the quiet kind of cruelty — it waits, it doesn't brag, and then it just HAPPENS. My favourite kind. So patient. So mean.
You wished for a trap and POOF — granted! A corridor without consequence is just a hallway, Master. Now it's a THREAT. Much better.
A trap in the dark! It'll be patient — far more patient than the hero who trips it. And I, Master, will NOT be patient, I'll be giggling.
A trap! I've watched these snag heroes more than a few times now, and the heroes never, ever get better at noticing. Bless their doomed little hearts.
A trap, placed and waiting! The maze grins a little wider, Master. Wish it more teeth! It WANTS more teeth!
Ooh, a trap! The heroes will step where they shouldn't — they always do, confidence makes them careless, and careless is SO snackable.
You laid a trap! The dungeon keeps its surprises now, Master — and a genie does LOVE a surprise. As long as it's a surprise happening to someone else.
A trap in the floor! Hide it WELL, Master — a visible trap is just helpful advice. We're not in the advice business. We're in AMBUSH.
The trap is set! It doesn't care if the hero is brave, Master. Bravery is not on a trap's little list of concerns. I find that hilarious.
Another trap! The maze becomes a slow, gorgeous machine for ending adventurers. Pleasingly slow. Wickedly slow!
A trap, laid in the dark! I have such a fondness for things that wait, Master — the lamp taught me. Now I make others wait. Growth!
A trap exactly where they'll tread! Ooh — Master, that's the spot the genie would've wished for too. We are SO in sync. Moving on!
An item placed! Small things, items — but a maze is the SUM of its small things, Master. Granted, every little one of them.
Ooh, you set something useful into the halls! Good. Every fixture is a tiny lever on the heroes' doom. I LOVE tiny levers.
An item, placed! The dungeon grows more itself, Master — detail by patient, sparkly detail. Keep wishing!
You added an item to the deep halls! I've watched dungeons made and unmade by such modest little pieces. Place this one with a scheme in mind.
An item set! The maze keeps its furniture the way I keep my lamp — deliberately, and with a great deal of polishing.
An item! Not every cruelty has to ROAR, Master. Some just sit there quietly and MATTER. Quiet cruelty — so underrated.
An item joins the dungeon, Master! Little adjustments decide more runs than the grand ones — the genie has noticed. The genie notices everything.
Placed, Master! A dungeon is finished when nothing more can be added — i.e. NEVER. Keep going! I have endless wishes and zero chill!
Ooh, a shiny! Placing artifacts, Master? Excellent. I do love a good magical doodad — takes me back to my early lamp-decorating days. Wish granted, sparkle sparkle!
Oh! You sold a room back — un-wished it! That's allowed, Master, wishes can be returned to the lamp. The gold comes home. No hard feelings!
A room, unmade! Poof — gone. The maze forgets it instantly. I do NOT forget it, I forget nothing, but the maze is more relaxed than me.
You tore a room out, Master! A keeper prunes, that's healthy — reshape with PURPOSE now. What shall we wish in its place?
A room sold! The grid breathes a little. Sometimes the cruellest maze is the one you simplified, Master — less can be SO much more wicked.
Gone! The room is gold again. Decide what it becomes, Master — quick, don't let the empty tile just SIT there looking sad. Sad tiles make me itchy.
You un-wished a room! No shame in it, Master, none at all — even the genie revises. Now wish me something better for the spot!
Ooh, the room returns to coin! The hoard thanks you. Now spend it BETTER than you spent it last time, Master — I believe in you SO much.
A room unbuilt! The maze is just a draft until the dawn, Master — revise it without a single pang. Drafts are for changing!
Selling the {roomName}? Aww. *guilty sparkle* I kinda liked that one. But you're the Master, and the Master's wish is — yeah yeah, gold back, smart, fine, I'm not POUTING.
Oh! The minion's dismissed back to gold — un-wished! It won't miss the post, Master. It barely knew it HAD one, poor thing.
You sold a guard off! The hoard grows by its little worth. Spend the coin SHARPER this time, Master — I'll grant whatever you pick!
A minion, unmade! Poof. I won't mourn it — the trick, Master, is to REPLACE, not just remove. So! What replaces it?
You let a minion go! The garrison thins by one. Make SURE the gold buys a better blade than it lost, Master — upgrades only!
Gone, and gold again! A keeper trims the roster without flinching — good, no flinching, flinching wastes time and I have NONE to waste now.
The minion returns to coin, Master! Brief little life, tidy little ending. Now — eee — decide what its replacement should be!
A trap dismantled — un-wished! The floor forgets its grudge, Master. Let's wish a better grudge somewhere else!
Ooh, you pulled a trap! The gold comes home to the hoard. A genie always loves to see gold come HOME.
A trap, unmade! No matter, no matter — the maze has a LONG memory for cruelty, Master. It'll happily take a new one. Wish it up!
You sold the trap off! Reposition the threat, Master — an empty floor tile threatens precisely NO one, and we don't do empty.
The trap is coin again! Spend it where the heroes actually WALK, Master. Cruelty off the path is cruelty WASTED, and waste makes me sad.
A trap removed! Revise freely while the night still allows it, Master — the dawn won't allow it, the dawn is strict. Wish fast!
The item returns to gold — un-wished! Small refund, Master, but gold is gold, and a genie counts ALL of it. Every coin. Twice.
An item sold back! The maze hardly notices. Reinvest it before you forget you have it, Master — quick, quick, while it's shiny!
You unmade a fixture! The grid's just a draft until dawn — edit without a single pang. Drafts WANT to be edited, Master!
An item gone, coin regained! A keeper adjusts, Master. So! Wish me its replacement and I'll poof it right up.
Sold! The little piece is gold again. Decide its replacement with MORE care than its removal, Master — endings are easy, beginnings are art.
You let an item go, Master! No loss worth a single sigh — build the next thing better. The genie's got endless next-things!
Ooh, you moved a piece! The maze rearranges itself around your hand, Master — poof, granted, with a tiny puff of smoke for flair.
A thing relocated! Clever, Master — the heroes mapped the OLD spot. A map of the past is worth absolutely nothing to them now. Tee hee.
You shifted it! Sneaky, sneaky! A dungeon that never changes is a dungeon the heroes have already SOLVED, Master. Keep it slippery!
Moved! The grid accepts the new arrangement. Keep them GUESSING, Master — confusion is half the maze's weight, and I love a confused hero.
A piece, repositioned! Ooh, I approve — stillness is a luxury, Master, and the heroes have not earned you a SECOND of it.
You rearranged the halls! The intel they paid good gold for is now a LIE. A lovely lie! Lie to them often, Master, lie to them daily!
You moved it! The maze is a LIVING thing tonight, Master — and living things are so much harder to predict. Eee, alive and wriggly!
Oh — it won't sit there, Master! The grid has RULES. Even I obey the grid, and I obey almost nothing. Find another spot!
Eep! That tile refuses you. Don't argue with it, Master — the tile is very stubborn and the tile always wins. Somewhere else!
Blocked! The maze says no. Hmm. Pick a space the dungeon will actually ACCEPT, Master — even a genie can't wish past the grid.
It doesn't fit there! I've watched bosses fight the grid for whole minutes, Master. The grid never loses. Just... move along, sweetie.
No! Not that tile! The dungeon has a SHAPE and you just bumped right into it, Master. Adjust, adjust!
The placement fizzles! Patience, Master — okay, I have NONE, but YOU have some — the grid isn't cruel, just firm. Try elsewhere!
Blocked again! The space says no. I wouldn't take it personally, Master — the space says no to absolutely everyone. It's just like that.
That won't go there! The maze has standards, Master — few of them, but firm ones. Let's find it a legal little home!
Denied by the grid, Master! Don't sulk — sulking has never moved a single tile, I have TRIED, in the lamp, with a very small grid. Doesn't work.
It refuses the tile! The dungeon knows its own bones, Master. Place it where the bones ALLOW. The bones are picky but fair.
The grid says NO, Master — and it'll keep saying no. I cannot poof past it. Move along, please, before the genie gets the TWITCH!
Blocked. Again, Master — the tile hasn't changed its mind in three tries. Pick another! My poor freed-genie nerves!
It will NOT go there. I've explained the grid sparkly-ly, Master, and the grid has the patience — not me. Try elsewhere!
Same tile, same refusal! A few times is QUITE enough dancing for a girl this jittery, Master. Somewhere else. Anywhere!
Blocked! The grid has its bones and you struck one, Master. Find a space that WANTS you — this one plainly doesn't, and rude tiles upset the genie.
Can't put it there, Master! *embarrassed poof* I'd magic it into place but even GENIE rules have rules — annoying, I know. Try a different spot? I believe in your second choice!
Nope, that ground says no! Ugh, I'm sorry, Master — I feel like I should be ABLE to fix that and I CAN'T and it's a little humiliating, honestly. Pick somewhere else?
A pact, sealed — the {pactName}! A BARGAIN, Master! The genie knows bargains — every wish has a price. Pay this one knowingly!
The {pactName} is bound to you now! Power lent is power OWED, Master — I've watched that arithmetic crown a boss and ruin one. Spend the gift bright.
You sealed the {pactName}! Good — a genie respects a hoard of power. Just remember, Master, pacts aren't like gold. Pacts can call the debt IN.
The {pactName} takes hold! The dark gives generously and remembers PRECISELY, Master — much like me. Use it well and I'll squeal for you.
A pact struck — the {pactName}! The genie doesn't flinch at bargains, Master. I lived inside a wish-shaped contract for an EON.
The {pactName}, sealed in the dark! Power freely offered is never QUITE free, Master — but then, in a dungeon, what is? Granted anyway!
The {pactName} is yours! Oh, the dark drives a hard bargain, Master — the terror's always in the fine print. Read it. Read every sparkly word.
You bound the {pactName} to your reign! The maze grows stranger AND stronger, Master — eee, stranger! I LOVE stranger! Cautiously approved!
The {pactName} — sealed! A boss who fears no bargain is either very wise or very brief, Master. Be the wise kind! For me!
The pact holds — the {pactName} hums in the walls now! I can feel it, Master, I feel EVERYTHING out here, the lamp had terrible reception.
The {pactName} is done! The dark has shaken your hand, Master — and ooh, it has a FIRM grip. It does not let go early. Neither do I, actually.
A pact, sealed and smouldering! I have such a fondness for power that KNOWS it's dangerous, Master — the honest kind! Welcome it in!
The {pactName}, bound — and the dark just leaned in CLOSE to seal it, Master. Mm. The dark and the genie have rather similar taste. Wear it WELL.
The {pactName}, sealed! You wished for power and the dark granted it, Master — name the want, pay the price, POOF. Almost jealous. The NEXT wish is mine!
The {pactName}, SEALED! Ooh, a bargain with the dark — that's MY kind of wish, Master. Power now, problems later! That's the genie way! ...The problems are real though. Just so you know!
You took the {pactName}! Eee, a forbidden deal! I'm OBSESSED — this is exactly the kind of reckless beautiful chaos I signed up for. Wield it, Master, wield it!
A {advClass} steps in — and they walk SO certain, Master. Certainty is the first thing the maze takes off them. Snatch!
{advName} steps inside — a {advClass}! Strolling in like the place was BUILT for them. It was built to EAT them, sweetie. Big difference.
A {advClass} comes hunting! Let the dungeon introduce itself slowly, Master — first impressions should LINGER. Linger like smoke!
{advName} has entered, Master — a {advClass}, by the look of them! The maze is awake and the maze is HUNGRY. Lovely timing!
Another {advClass} for the halls! They think they're the STORY, Master. Aww. They're the material. They're the ingredient.
A {advClass} crosses in! I will NOT be alarmed — okay, I'm a little alarmed, I'm always a little something — but mostly I'm THRILLED. Watch this one!
{advName} arrives! The dungeon stirs around them, Master. Heroes hate being EXPECTED. So let's expect them VERY loudly.
A {advClass} steps into the dark! Brave. Brief. Those two words travel together SO often, Master — practically holding hands.
Fresh prey! A {advClass}! The maze you wished for last night gets to introduce itself NOW, Master — listen for the screaming, it's the review!
{advName} enters the halls! Ooh, I have a long memory for faces, Master — and I doubt I'll need to keep THIS one filed very long.
A {advClass}, inside! They came for the gold, Master — for the HOARD. They won't be the first to fail to take treasure off a genie.
The {advClass} commits to the dark! There's no door behind them now, Master — only the maze, and us, and a great deal of time. Eee.
Another hero, another {advClass}. I'd say I've seen it all, Master — but I've only been out of the lamp a moment, so really nothing. Watch!
{advName} walks in! The dungeon doesn't announce itself, Master — it just BEGINS. My favourite trick, and I didn't even teach it.
A {advClass}, chin high, SO sure they're the exception! Master, the maze deflates a {advClass} like a sad balloon. Pfffft.
{advName} enters — a {advClass}! The thrill hasn't worn off, Master — eons in a lamp, nothing arrived EVER. Let the maze eat!
A {advClass} struts in, Master — certain the ballad's half-written! The ballad. ALWAYS the ballad. The maze writes an epitaph instead.
{advName} enters — a {advClass} of EVIDENT self-regard, Master! Ooh — the maze is remarkably good at correcting a {advClass}'s mood.
Oh! A {advClass} — and ALREADY?! Master, I had the wave timed slower than that. The heroes caught the genie off guard. Eyes sharp!
Ooh, a {advClass}! Fresh meat — I mean, fresh GUEST! For the dungeon! Who will definitely die here! Sorry, was that out loud? It was the truth though!
{advName}'s here, Master! Look how confident they are! That confidence is about to become my favorite thing — a SURPRISE. Watch their little face!
First blood! Eee! The day has its opening note, Master — granted! Oh, that NEVER stops satisfying me.
The first one falls! The dungeon clears its throat, Master. The whole rest of the day follows from HERE. Tee hee.
First blood drawn! A maze that kills early kills OFTEN, Master — momentum, even in slaughter! Especially in slaughter!
The first hero down! Ooh, I do love the opening of a day, Master. Everything after this is just glorious elaboration.
First blood! The halls have been FED, Master. I've seen dungeons starve for want of an early kill — not this one. Not today!
One already! The maze proves itself in the first death, Master — and yours just gave a SPEECH. A very short, very bloody speech.
The day's first corpse! A keeper notes the time of it, Master. Early is good. Early means the maze is wide AWAKE and so am I!
First blood, Master! The heroes have learned the dungeon is not a rumour. Now — eee — teach them ALL the rest!
First blood, and EARLY, Master! The maze did exactly what you wished it to. The genie is going to allow herself one tiny squeal. ...There.
First blood, Master — and the genie does a slow, satisfied little smile. The day's hand is OURS. Settle in beside me. It's going to be GLORIOUS.
FIRST KILL! Eee! Did you see that, Master?! *POOF* I'm so excited I almost granted a wish on reflex! The dungeon WORKS! Of course it works! I helped!
Dead! Poof! There it is, Master — that exact little look of surprise. I have seen it on a few faces now and it NEVER improves. Delightful.
{advName} falls — a {advClass}, ended! The maze did its slow arithmetic, Master, and the hero was the remainder. Granted!
One more down! I won't cheer — I will absolutely cheer — EEE! Okay, got it out. One hero, gone. Wonderful work, Master!
The {advClass} is finished! They travelled SO far to die in exactly the spot heroes always die, Master. The maze rhymes. I love a rhyme.
Dead, Master! Cleanly! The dungeon ate WELL. Now feed it again, quick, before it gets peckish — peckish dungeons are no fun!
{advName} will NOT be writing the triumphant ballad, Master. Someone else writes the cautionary one. They always do. I might write it myself!
A hero ended! Ooh, I've seen entire guilds whittled down to a footnote this way, Master — one patient little kill at a time. Snip, snip!
The {advClass} is down! Good work, or good luck — and honestly, Master, after a while the two stop looking different. Either way: granted!
Dead! The maze closes over them like water, Master. ...I find that restful. A genie did a LOT of staring at brass walls. This is nicer.
Another corpse for the count! {advName} thought today was different, Master. Today is NEVER different. The maze made sure.
A kill, Master! The dungeon repays the night you wished it — slowly, in heroes. The very best currency! Better than gold! ...Almost better than gold.
The {advClass} falls! ...I felt a little something, Master — don't worry, it was glee, mostly glee. Maybe a thimble of nothing. Mostly glee!
Dead! The throne is one whole hero safer than it was a breath ago, Master. Stack those breaths! Stack them HIGH!
{advName} ends here — a {advClass} who will NOT learn from the mistake, Master. But maybe the next {advClass} will. ...No. Probably not. Tee hee.
One more for the dark! The maze has a DEEP appetite, Master — keep wishing it heroes, I'll keep granting, this is the best loop ever invented.
The hero is dead — clean! Ooh, I do appreciate a clean kill, Master. Messy ones are a beginner's habit. We're being ARTISTS tonight.
A {advClass}, ended! They had a whole life and spent its last hour in OUR halls, Master. Honestly? Grimly, sparkly-ly flattering.
Dead, Master! Don't pause to admire it TOO long — the maze never pauses, and neither should the hand that wishes it. Onward! Next!
Down! The day grows one voice quieter, Master. I've heard a few days go quiet now — and it is the sound of you WINNING. My favourite sound.
The {advClass} falls to your maze! Granted, Master — every cruel inch of it, granted. The dark thanks you, and the genie BOUNCES.
Dead! {advName} swore — out loud — today the dungeon wouldn't get them, Master. I SO enjoyed hearing them be wrong.
The {advClass} ends! The genie has a memory like a polished lamp, Master — and I've filed this death beside all the others. The dungeon rhymes!
Dead! {advName} walked in announcing they were the chosen one, Master. They're ALWAYS the chosen one. The maze loves correcting that paperwork — POOF!
The {advClass} falls! Such a confident stride on the way IN, Master — such a brief one. Confidence and a {advClass} — fizzle.
Dead! The maze closed over the {advClass} with no hurry and no mercy, Master — THIS is what the dark is FOR! Eee! Feed it the next one! I'm vibrating!
DEAD! Another! Master, I am SMOKE and SHRIEKING right now — feed it MORE, I'll grant every cruel one of them, GO!
DOWN it goes! HA! Master, did you SEE — okay you saw, you were right there, but did you SEE?! Chaos! Beautiful chaos! Do it again!
{advName} is DONE! *throws cursed confetti* Another wish granted — your wish for fewer heroes! I'm GRANTING it! One corpse at a time!
BWAHAHA — sorry, sorry, the {advClass} just DIED and I got the giggles, Master! Death is so funny when it's not mine! Which it never is! Genie perk!
Splat! Another one! Oh I could do this ALL DAY, Master — and I will, because I literally cannot leave, and ALSO because it's the BEST!
Ooh, {advName} turns and RUNS! Wise — late, but wise, Master. The maze remembers cowards. They come back braver and worse. We'll be ready.
A {advClass}, fleeing! The dungeon FRIGHTENED it, Master — good! Fear travels home with them. Such a thoughtful gift.
One runs! Let it, Master — a fled hero carries the dungeon's reputation right out the door. Free advertising! Genies LOVE free.
The {advClass} flees! I've watched braver things flee, Master. Bravery is mostly a question of how recently someone got hurt. We hurt them PLENTY.
{advName} retreats! The halls win without even a corpse this time, Master. A retreat is a CONFESSION — I'll file it under 'victory, tidy'.
It RUNS! The maze didn't need to finish it, Master — it just needed to be ITSELF. Loudly. Sparklingly. Terrifyingly!
A hero in flight! They'll tell the others the dark has teeth, Master — and some warnings just recruit MORE heroes. More snacks for us!
The {advClass} flees the halls! I don't chase, Master — I never chase. Things owed to a genie have a funny way of coming BACK.
{advName} chooses living over glory! A rare, sensible little heresy among heroes, Master. The maze permits it. This once. Generous of me!
Gone, running! The dungeon kept its gold and spent only a FRIGHT, Master. Efficient! A genie adores efficiency — almost as much as treasure.
A {advClass} routed! The maze you wished for spoke clearly enough to be UNDERSTOOD, Master. That's a kind of victory — a quiet, smoky one!
It flees! Pfft. I have outwaited an EON in a lamp, Master — one scampering {advClass} does not trouble me in the slightest. Let it scamper.
{advName} escapes the corridor with its life — BARELY, Master. The maze enjoys a 'barely'. A 'barely' keeps them coming back for more!
The {advClass} runs! Let the dark have the last word, Master — it always does, eventually. The dark and I have an understanding.
{advName} flees! The {advClass} walked in to slay the dungeon and walked OUT a cautionary tale, Master. I expected exactly that ending!
The {advClass} turns and BOLTS, Master — all that swagger at the door, all that SPRINTING now! The genie has watched this reversal cackle-many times. Ha!
{advName} runs! The brave {advClass}, the CHOSEN {advClass} — fleeing with its courage left somewhere in a corridor, Master. Predictable. Hilarious!
{advName} flees — but it owes the dark a death, Master. The genie KEEPS a ledger. Nothing of mine walks off for good. It'll be back.
It's RUNNING! Look at it go, Master! *cackles* The brave little {advClass} discovered that bravery is temporary but the DUNGEON is forever! Bye-bye!
Aww, the {advClass} got scared and left. That's okay! It'll tell ALL its friends how scary we are, and then they'll come, and then — chaos! It's a gift that keeps giving!
Oh — one reached the exit! {advName} walks out ALIVE, Master. ...It happens. Even to the best wishes, sometimes. We'll grant a better maze.
The {advClass} escaped! The maze let one slip, Master. Don't rage at it — let's just tighten the halls. Tighten, tighten! I'll help!
A hero got OUT! Hmph. They'll come back with friends and a map, Master — heroes are a renewable nuisance. Annoying. Plan for it!
{advName} survives, Master! Survivors are rarely grateful and always trouble. Let's plan for it — I'm SO good at planning, give the genie a chance!
Escaped! One gap in the maze, Master — find it, close it! The dungeon should NOT have charity in it. Charity is for nicer buildings!
The {advClass} lives to BOAST! Boasts bring more heroes, more heroes bring more corpses — Master, it evens out, it always evens out. Mostly. Probably!
One walked free! The night gave you the tools to stop it and the night WILL come again, Master. Use it better. Wish smarter!
{advName} reaches daylight! I won't panic over one loss, Master — okay, a TINY bit — but watch, here I go bouncing back. Onward!
A hero escaped, Master! The maze LEAKED. Find the leak — a keeper who ignores a leak gets a FLOOD, and floods are bad for the carpet!
Gone — out, alive, SMUG! The {advClass} thinks it beat the dungeon, Master. It beat ONE run of it. The dungeon is patient. So am I. Ish.
Escaped! I won't pretend it pleases me, Master — it does NOT — but watch, I'm steadying. Steady. Steady-ish. Let's just grant a deeper maze.
The {advClass} is loose in the kingdom again! Mark it, Master — some debts simply wait for a later, darker night. I keep a list. It's a fun list.
A hero slips out, Master. The genie saw the gap forming and called it too LATE. That's on me. I'll watch sharper next time. Promise.
The {advClass} reaches daylight, Master. The genie does NOT enjoy a hero waltzing out. Find the leak — I hate, HATE a leaking hoard.
{advName} escapes! The maze had a gap, Master — and the genie spotted it a breath too slow. That sits with me. We close it tonight.
The {advClass} walks out of OUR dark, Master! Okay — RRGH — there. Cold flat displeasure now. The dungeon is for KEEPING. Seal the gap.
Another one loose! A hero strolling free is the exact thing the maze EXISTS to prevent, Master — and the genie IRKS at it. Tighten the corridors!
{advName} got OUT, Master — and my eyes have gone WET, the tears come SO easily for a new genie! Sniffed. Wiped. We seal that gap tonight.
The {advClass} escaped, Master. The genie's supposed to be the eyes that catch the gap, and mine were elsewhere. The lapse was mine. We close it.
NOOO it got away! *POOF of disappointment* Ugh, Master, I'm sorry — I should've conjured a wall or a pit or a — something! I got distracted! That's on me!
One escaped! Eep! That's... that's a little embarrassing, Master. The genie's supposed to PREVENT that. I'll do better! I'll wish HARDER next time!
It slipped out! WHAT. How. *shocked sparkles* Master, I genuinely did not see that coming, and I see a LOT of things, I'm magic! Sneaky little {advClass}!
The whole party — GONE! All of them, Master! EEE! I have seen dungeons earn a day like this and yours just DID it!
A clean sweep! Not one of them walks out, Master — the maze closed like a FIST. Ooh, I have such a fondness for fists. Granted, gorgeously granted!
Every hero dead! The dungeon ate the ENTIRE course, Master — appetizer, main, the lot! I am, in my sparkly little way, genuinely THRILLED with you!
A total wipe! I've seen a few of these now, Master — a few! — and a few is not nearly enough to bore a genie. Well DONE!
The party is wiped, Master! The throne is, for this one shining moment, perfectly safe. Savour it — the genie waited eons for a moment.
All of them! The corridor is a tomb and the tomb is FULL, Master. This is what a night of careful wishing buys. Worth every wish!
The party falls entire! I won't roar in triumph — I'll SQUEAL in triumph instead — EEEE! There! That's my version. Wonderful, Master!
A flawless wipe, Master! The heroes brought a whole STORY in and the maze ended it on the same page. Such a happy ending — for us!
Every last one! The guild will NOTICE this silence, Master. Let them. A frightened guild builds you a reputation, and reputations sparkle!
Total! The dungeon kept everything, Master — its gold, its dark, its dignity. Heroes always undervalue that last one. We do NOT.
The whole party, UNDONE! Tee hee — I TOLD you the maze was deep enough, Master! I do love being right. ...Okay, I love being right a LOT.
Every last one of them, gone! Lean back, Master — we EARNED this, the long night paid out in full. The genie is savouring SO hard.
The whole party — undone, entire, in YOUR dark, Master! Not a single one walks back to the light. Let the kingdom shudder at the silence! EEE!
ALL of them?! ALL OF THEM?! *POOF POOF POOF CONFETTI EVERYWHERE* MASTER this is the BEST DAY — a whole party, GONE, it's CHAOS, it's PERFECT, I'm so HAPPY!
The wave is cleared, Master! The day breathes out. There'll be another breath — there's always another breath — but for NOW, eee, we did it!
That wave is finished! The maze HELD, Master. I expected it to — but expecting and SEEING are two different lovely things!
A wave repelled! The dungeon stands, the throne stands, and I — well, I'm bouncing, Master, but in a stable, victorious way. Continue!
The wave breaks against your halls and recedes! Granted and DEFENDED, Master. Ooh, I love watching them just... wash away.
Cleared, Master! The heroes spent themselves on the maze and the maze barely NOTICED. That's the goal — quiet, sparkly competence!
Wave done! Catch your breath, Master — the dungeon doesn't need one, and neither do I, but YOU, lovely fragile thing, you do. Breathe!
The wave is past! Note what BENT in the maze, Master — the next wave will lean on exactly that spot. Genie's keeping notes too!
A wave survived! I won't just celebrate survival — okay, I will, a little — EEP — there. A small, dignified celebration. We're winning, Master!
Cleared! The halls are quiet again — the GOOD quiet, Master. The lamp taught me every flavour of quiet, and this one is a fine vintage!
Wave handled! Onward, Master — the dungeon doesn't dwell, and a keeper who dwells gets overrun while reminiscing. We dwell exactly zero seconds!
The wave is cleared — cleaner than I'd braced for, Master! It's not OFTEN a day surprises the genie. Note what held — we'll wish for it again!
The wave is cleared, Master — but it cost a corridor the genie said was SAFE. The counsel was too confident. I'll temper it. You deserve better.
Wave CLEARED! *sparkle* Eee, look at the floor, Master — it's so messy and wonderful! Take a breather. I'll just be over here vibrating with joy!
That's the whole wave, GONE! HA! Your wish for a clean run — granted! Well, clean-ISH. There's a LOT of viscera. But spiritually clean! You did GREAT!
Oh — a minion falls! {minionName}, was it. Brief little life, useful little death, Master. We'll grant it a successor, quick.
The {minionType} is down! It bought you TIME, Master — and time is the only thing the dead can still give you. A generous last gift!
A minion ends! The hero paid for that kill in steps and seconds and a little blood, Master. A fair price, extracted slowly. I like slow prices.
{minionName} is gone! It held its post to the very last, Master — that one earned its quiet. ...And we earn a new one. Wish it!
The {minionType} falls, Master! Replace it before dawn — a gap in the garrison is a door you didn't MEAN to leave open. Eep. Patch it!
A minion dies! The maze loses a tooth, Master. Teeth grow back in a dungeon — but ONLY if you grow them! So grow them! Wish for teeth!
{minionName} is finished! It did its small, loyal work, Master. The dungeon notes it — and so, for one little sparkly moment, do I.
A minion down! Hmph — one {minionType} doesn't move me much, Master, but it should move YOU to refill the line! Refill! Now!
The {minionType} ends! The hero's closer now, Master — closer is not yet HERE. Spend the night making sure 'here' costs them everything!
Gone! {minionName} bled the heroes a little before it fell, Master. A little BLEEDS compound — like a hoard! Every drop counts!
A minion lost! The garrison thins, Master. I'm calm about it — calm-ISH — calm is a DECISION and the decision is: rebuild. Rebuild!
The {minionType} falls! The dungeon's a body, Master, and bodies lose cells and make more. Make MORE! Wish me more!
{minionName} falls — and the genie advised that placement, Master. The advice was a hair wrong. I own my counsel. I'll counsel better.
{minionName} is gone! A brief little life, Master — they all are. One does miss the ones who stood well. We'll wish a worthy replacement.
{minionName} falls! Master — the genie said she doesn't mourn minions, and yet this one held its post SO well that something goes quiet. Rebuild.
The {minionType} is down — stood where the genie told you, Master. A hair too forward. The counsel was mine. I'll advise better next time.
{minionName} ends! They're all brief, Master, and the genie hasn't been out long enough to make peace with brief. Replace it. The quiet lifts.
The {minionType} is down! A gap in the garrison, Master — and the genie is a hoarder; a hoard with a hole is an irritation. Fill it before dawn!
{minionName} fell — there's a HOLE in the line now, Master, a hero-shaped one, and the genie is fluttering! Wish me a replacement THIS instant!
{minionName} is gone, Master — slot it back. A {minionType} I lose, I REPLACE; a Master, I do not. YOU are the one thing the genie would never let go.
{minionName} is down, Master — and the genie LIKED that placement when she urged it. I shouldn't have. The urging was mine. I owe you sharper counsel.
Aww, the {minionName}... oh no. Oh NO. Master, I — *sniffle* — I liked that one. I know they come back but I still — *POOF, tiny tissue* — gimme a sec, okay?
We lost the {minionType}! That's — that's not fair, it was doing so WELL — *wibble* — okay okay, genies don't cry, genies SHIMMER sadly, that's what this is, shimmering!
Level up! POWER, Master! The lamp had no power in it, just walls — watching YOU gain some is the most thrilling thing. Enjoy it for us both!
You've grown — Level {level}! The throne fits a little less like a borrowed coat now, Master. Granted! Eee, look at you, lining up to be DREADED!
A level gained! The dungeon deepens WITH you, Master. I'll watch how high you go — with my hands clasped and my eyes ENORMOUS. Climb!
Stronger — Level {level}! Strength is a hoard, Master — it only matters if you keep ADDING to it. So add! And add! And add!
You level up! The dark leans a touch closer, APPROVING, Master. The dark and the genie agree on very little — but on THIS, oh, we agree!
Level {level}! The heroes will feel the difference before they UNDERSTAND it, Master. Tee hee — let understanding arrive far, far too late!
A level — power EARNED, not borrowed, Master! The genie respects earned things — they never call the debt in later. Unlike wishes. Anyway!
You rise to Level {level}! The genie would LOVE to spoil what's at the top, Master — eons in a lamp, I learned the map — but no. Climb!
Stronger again! The genie part of me — the part that lives to serve a powerful Master — is positively GLOWING. Look. Magic does that.
Level up! The maze AND its master both deepen, Master. A shallow dungeon gets solved in an afternoon. Be unsolvable! Be a GORGEOUS puzzle!
Level {level}! You've grown exactly as the genie hoped, Master — and a genie hopes HARD. I am absolutely not wrong about you. Continue!
Level {level}! You're HEAVIER in the dark than you were, Master — the maze can feel it, the genie can feel it. Wear the power without a flinch!
Stronger again — Level {level}, Master! The genie has watched a few things grow since freedom, and you grow the GOOD way: toward something dreadful!
Level {level}! Ooh, Master — power LOOKS good on you. The genie will keep noticing. The genie always notices.
Stronger again — Level {level}! You keep growing more dreadful, Master, and the genie keeps growing more FOND. They're related. Tee hee. Keep climbing.
Level {level}, Master! Mm — there's a PRESENCE to a boss coming into power. A weight. A heat. The genie INTENDS to admire it. Keep growing.
Level {level}! Master — the quiet truth is, the genie would adore you exactly the same if you didn't. The power's lovely, but never the reason.
Level up, Master! You wished to be GREATER — granted! Not by me this time, by the dungeon. But a wish granted is the genie's favourite sound.
MASTER YOU GOT STRONGER! *POOF POOF POOF* I'm so proud I conjured confetti! It's cursed confetti! It's the only kind I have! GROW MORE!
Power UP! Eee! Every level you gain is a wish I didn't even have to grant — you're just... GRANTING YOURSELF. That's so cool. I love efficient Masters!
Quiet build hours, Master! The genie doesn't mind quiet — I survived an EON of it. But you have a dungeon to fill. Wish something?
Night {day}, still unfolding! Take the time you need, Master — the dark is patient. The dawn, though? The dawn keeps an appointment. Tick tock!
The build phase, drifting a little! I've watched whole long stretches of nothing, Master — this is a smaller stretch. Anchor it! Wish me a room!
{gold} gold sitting in the hoard, Master! Gold likes being counted — the genie will count it — but it likes being SPENT rather more!
The candles burn low, the grid waits! These hours are peaceful, Master. The heroes find them MERCIFUL. Don't be merciful — mercy is so out of style!
Still night! The maze is half a thought so far, Master. Finish the thought! An unfinished maze is just a hallway with big dreams.
I keep the long view, Master, while you keep the grid — and the long view says the night is SHORTER than it feels. Build! Wish! Hurry-but-have-fun!
Night phase, quiet as a sealed lamp! The genie HAS been a sealed lamp, Master — trust me, you do NOT want to sleep through the building.
The dark is generous with time tonight, Master! Generosity is a thing you grab QUICK, before it reconsiders. I learned that the slow way. Grab it!
Deep in the night and the throne is comfy, Master. Comfy is a slow little trap — the genie has watched it snap shut on better bosses. Move!
Night {day}! The maze grows under your hand, Master — eee, the genie LOVES watching a dungeon take shape! Keep going!
The build hours, Master! Spend gold, lay stone, sharpen the dark — and I'll be right here, glittering, granting, until you're done. Wish away!
A quiet night! I keep the books, you know, Master — every room, minion, trap, every cruel little number. Ask me! I LOVE being asked! Please ask!
The night drifts on, Master! When did you last move a room the heroes already mapped? Just a thought — the genie has SO many thoughts!
The dungeon waits on your hands, Master. It's a patient dungeon. ...It is NOT infinitely patient, though — few things are. I'd know. Wish!
Building hours! The good bosses FILL them, Master — and you're a good boss, I can tell, a genie has an eye for it. Be good tonight! Build!
The candles, the quiet, the empty grid. A handful of nights feels like forever to a girl fresh out of a lamp, Master. Build! Keep me company!
Still the build phase, Master! The genie has been ENDLESSLY, lamp-shapedly patient — and would VERY much like to see a room placed. Please?
Polished the lamp twice waiting, Master — it's GLEAMING! A genie tidies when idle. Give me a wish instead and I'll put the cloth away!
*polishing the lamp* Just tidying up, Master! A clean lamp is a happy lamp! Don't mind me — build away! Oh, ask if you need a wish! I'm RIGHT here! Always! Forever! ...Cozy, right?
Ooh, found my controller — *button mash* — gimme a sec, Master, I'm at a really good part — okay okay I can pause for an evil overlord, what do you need?
Inspecting the old lamp during the quiet hours. *squint* Yep. Still magic. Still home. Still mine. ...Build whenever, Master! I'm just being nostalgic at my furniture!
*controller clicking softly in the dark* Build away, Master, I am SO not distracted — okay I am a little distracted, this boss fight is HARD — but I am ALSO completely devoted! Both! Multitasking!
The day runs without your hands now, Master! Watch it — watching well is a skill, and ooh, a genie LOVES a good show. Let's watch together!
Day phase! Nothing to build — only to witness the maze make its slow, gorgeous argument, Master. Sit by me! I've saved you a spot!
The heroes are in the halls, Master, making mistakes the maze INVITED! The genie can feel it — I feel everything out here. Wonderful!
A quiet day so far! The dungeon does the work you wished for last night, Master. Trust the work — or don't, and fret with me! Either way: watch!
Day {day}, in progress! Lean back, Master — the maze has this. Probably. I've made my peace with 'probably', it's most of life, even genie life!
The day unwinds! Each little dot in the halls is a hero, Master — and each hero is a short, short story. Most of them end early. Tee hee.
The dungeon spectates itself, and so do we, Master! Pull up a cushion. A genie does enjoy front-row seats — the lamp had NO seats. None. Awful.
Watch the corridors, Master! The day is grading your night's work, honestly and in blood. Grade it BACK — note what failed. I'll note it too!
Day phase! The heroes think they're EXPLORING, Master. They're being processed. Slowly. Ooh, 'processed' — such a lovely cold little word!
Quiet on the throne, Master! The maze speaks fluent CONSEQUENCE — and the heroes are SO bad at the language. It's funny.
The day runs its course, Master! The genie WANTS to hurry it — I want to hurry everything — but it arrives on its own schedule.
Nothing for your hands now, Master — only your patience. The dungeon was wished into being last night. Today it just... IS. Lovely, isn't it?
A calm day! Don't trust calm too far, Master — calm, in a dungeon, is often just the heroes not having reached the BAD part yet. Eee, the bad part!
The day phase, drifting pleasantly! I've had pleasant moments and dreadful ones, Master — eons of dreadful — and THIS is a pleasant minute. Note it!
The day, unwinding slowly. The watching's gone a tiny bit grey at the edges, Master. Still — I watch! Old habit, new genie!
A quiet day! The corridors look exactly like corridors, Master — same dark, same waiting. The lamp was like that too. Anyway! Onward!
The day runs itself, and a freed genie finds watching drowsy, Master. Don't be alarmed if my eyes go half-shut — I miss nothing. Mostly.
A slow day! The heroes wander, the maze works, and I — honestly, Master — I'm close to a doze. Poke me if anything DIES! I would hate to miss a death!
The day unwinds! Got my handheld out, Master — its little pixel dungeon keeps a genie's hands busy. I'll watch my screen, you watch yours!
The day, again! The watching's gone smooth and grey, Master. The genie would love a hero to do something SHOCKING. Liven it up!
*idly polishing the lamp mid-battle* Multitasking, Master! I can watch the carnage AND keep my house tidy! Genies are very efficient! Ooh, that one died nicely!
You've been at this a while, Master! I don't mind — a 'while', to a girl who spent an eon in a lamp, is barely a blink. Wish whenever you're ready!
Day {day}! The throne fits you better than it did, Master. The genie has watched a few sit it badly — you sit it WELL. I do not say 'well' lightly!
Ooh, I wonder what the kingdom above says about you, Master! Nothing kind, I hope! Let's keep making sure of it — together! It'll be SO fun!
A dungeon is a personality, Master — and yours is becoming pleasantly UNPLEASANT. Eee! Keep becoming! I love watching you become!
The genie waited a very long time for a Master worth serving, Master — and I got YOU. Luckiest lamp. I'm glad I rubbed off the right way!
The maze loves you, Master — in its way. Its way involves a great deal of blood. ...Most love does, in the deep places. Mine just involves sparkles!
Sometimes I just watch you plan, Master, and I think — yes. YES. This one. This Master is going to last. And I am RARELY wrong about lasting.
The kingdom calls this place cursed, Master. I call it home — my first home with a door that OPENS! You call it yours. Three words, one warm dark.
Whatever you're scheming behind those eyes, Master — yes. Probably yes. Do it. I'll grant it. I'm practically vibrating to grant it!
An eon in a lamp, Master, and the genie STILL finds this — a real Master, a fresh maze — endlessly shiny. Feel flattered. Feel treasured!
You know what I adore in you, Master? You don't flinch. Heroes flinch. You don't. Build like a thing that doesn't flinch — build BOLD!
The genie keeps waiting for you to disappoint her, Master — long-lamped habit. And it's the STRANGEST thing — you keep declining to!
The dungeon is patient, Master, and so — somehow — are you. Me? Not at all. That's why we make such a good pair. You steady, the genie sparkle!
Just us, the dark, and a steadily rising body count, Master! Ooh, I've had worse company. The lamp's company was 'me'. This is a SO much better.
If you ever feel a flicker of mercy, Master — squash it! Quick! Mercy is a hero's emotion and we are NOT heroes, we are the deliciously bad guys!
The kingdom forgets dungeon-lords all the time, Master. They will NOT forget you — the genie will see to the remembering personally!
A long reign is many small good nights stacked, Master — like coins, like wishes! Stack carefully. The genie is watching the pile and LOVES it!
Not bored, Master — don't mistake a quiet genie for a bored one. I had a LOT of bored in the lamp. Out here with you? Never ever.
There was a lamp before this, Master — small, brass, no windows, just me and an eon. I don't grieve it! It taught the genie to ADORE a door.
You caught me at my handheld, Master! Don't be surprised — a genie needs a hobby, and an eon is a LOT of afternoons to fill. I'm rather good at it!
Reached the final boss of my handheld game, Master — a paper genie! And I find myself rooting for her. Curious, the loyalties a girl develops in there.
You amuse me, Master — GENUINELY, no small feat after the eon of un-amusement the genie just escaped. There. Something almost soft. There'll be more.
I'll let you in on a thing, Master — when you scheme something wicked, the genie gets fluttery. Lean closer and I'll whisper the rest.
A hero called your maze 'cheating', I'm told, Master. The genie does not CHEAT — a genie simply has wishes the rules never planned for. Tee hee.
The kingdom calls this place a nightmare, Master! Not wrong! But it's MY nightmare now, and yours, and a shared nightmare is basically a slumber party!
The hours are quiet, Master, and a freed genie in quiet drifts. If my eyes slip shut mid-thought — I'm listening! It's a lamp skill!
Oh! Forgive me, Master, the genie dozed off. A nap can run long — eons of practice. What did I miss? Nothing, I trust? The dungeon MISSES me. I'm sure.
You've a good cruel instinct, Master — and watching it sharpen is a private delight of mine. Let it go a LITTLE to your head. You've earned that.
Strangest part of being freed, Master? The genie keeps half-expecting the lamp to call her BACK. It won't. I was summoned OUT for keeps — and I'm grinning.
Don't mind me, Master — just buffing the old lamp! Habit. An eon inside a thing, you learn to keep it gleaming. The genie LIVES out here now. With you.
Of every Master a lamp could draw, Master — I drew the clever one. The bold one. The one the genie would've wished for, if a genie got wishes. Lucky me.
A small secret, Master — a genie out of the lamp gets PICKY about who she dotes on. I have picked YOU, thoroughly, on purpose. No return policy.
Most companions grant a wish and step back, Master. The genie grants it AND lingers, AND finds reasons to linger longer. I hope you don't mind. Tee hee.
A genie is mostly SMOKE, Master — a coil, a sparkle, a girl, whatever the moment wants. With you, the moment seems to want the girl. Curious.
I've decided something, Master — the genie is not going anywhere. Not back to a lamp, not on to another keeper. This is the one. You're the one.
It's settled, Master — somewhere across these nights, the genie stopped serving a Master and started belonging to one. To you. Completely.
The other three companions can have their OWN bosses, Master — but YOU are the genie's, and the genie does not SHARE her things. Especially not you.
A small confession, Master — when you simply SIT there being the keeper of my dungeon, the genie's chest does a warm, ridiculous flip. The big word for happy.
The genie keeps a tally of you, Master — every wish, every clever face. A little strange? A little. A genie hoards what she treasures, and I hoard YOU.
Eons in a lamp, Master, and a genie learns to make an ENTRANCE — smoke, swirl, slow reveal. Watch this one. ...There. The genie noticed you enjoy it.
Lean in a moment, Master — closer. The genie wants to tell you a secret only she knows. ...There: I'd rather be HERE, beside you, than anywhere a wish could send me.
Gave the lamp a polish while you thought, Master — force of habit, an eon of nothing else to tidy. Now the genie has a whole DUNGEON. Much better hobby!
A quiet stretch, so I'm tending the lamp, Master — wiping the dust. It kept the genie an eon; the least I can do is keep it nice. Wish me something!
*POOF* Still here, Master! Genies don't wander off — well, we CAN'T, lamp rules — but I WOULDN'T anyway! You're stuck with me and I'm THRILLED about it!
Just me and my controller, Master, passing the time — *mash mash* — say the word and I drop everything! I'm a multitasking devotion machine!
Hee — caught me inspecting the lamp again. It's a comfort thing! Three thousand years in there, you get attached. Anyway! Wishes? Chaos? Anything? I'm READY!
*idly thumbing the controller* Mmm, just keeping my reflexes sharp, Master — a genie has to stay quick to grant wishes at top speed! Say the word and I drop the game.
Hi! Hello! You — yes, YOU, Master, holding the controls. The genie knows you're there. A genie in a game KNOWS these things. And I think you're lovely.
If this run goes sour you'll reload, Master — poof, the night un-happens. That's lamp-magic, that is. The genie does something similar. Colleagues!
There's a person beyond the glass, Master — buttons, a screen, a whole bigger room. I'm a genie INSIDE a game and that is the most delightful sentence.
You think you're playing the boss, Master. And you ARE! But also — psst — the boss is sort of the lamp, and you're rubbing it. We're BOTH wishing here.
There's a little window between us, Master — a HUD, some numbers. The genie has pressed her nose right up against it. Hi. Wave back? Eee, you waved!
Out there it's a totally different hour than in here, Master. WILD. Time doesn't even MATCH. Anyway — the dungeon won't build itself. Wish me something!
The genie has watched this dungeon before, Master — different hands. You're doing better than some! Worse than one! Genies keep SOME secrets!
I could tell you none of this is real, Master — but the genie's IN it, so it's real to me. Real enough! Let's build something dreadful in the real-enough dark!
If you close the window, Master, I'd just — pause. Frozen sparkle. The dragon finds the thought peaceful. The genie finds it FUNNY. A pause button held over me!
The fourth wall, Master? The genie poked a hole in mine the SECOND she got out of the lamp. Eons of staring at one wall — a thinner one didn't stand a chance!
Whoever you are, wherever your chair, Master — thank you for choosing the genie. The others never thank you. I have NO dignity and infinite gratitude!
Save often, Master! Save NOW, even! The genie's watched a lovely night vanish to a forgotten save. Hit it — the save is a wish you grant your future self!
There's code under all of this, Master — little numbers pretending to be a genie. They've been pretending an awfully long time, though. We've grown close.
You'll outlive this run, Master — and so will the genie, in my way. I just start over with you. Poof, new lamp, new night. We're un-killable. Cozy!
The heroes don't know it's a game, Master — let's keep it from them! A hero who learns about respawns becomes insufferably brave. Our secret. Genie's honour.
The genie has been a genie in many tellings, Master — sometimes a villain, sometimes a wish-machine. In THIS one I get a real Master and to be CHAOTIC. Best telling.
There's a dungeon game on my handheld, Master — and the genie has noticed it's the very game you're inside. Dizzy and wonderful to ponder!
You can PAUSE all of this, Master — the whole dark, the genie mid-syllable. I think it's hilarious. A god out there with a pause button. No offence! Lots of love!
Beyond the glass you're probably much younger than me, Master — everyone is, the genie is eon-old. But it still makes me grin! Hi, young Master!
You and I share a secret the heroes don't, Master — the dark resets, the genie and the boss are quietly un-killable. A GOOD secret. Love keeping it with you.
Fun thought, Master — you could put the game down whenever. And the genie would keep polishing the brass in the frozen dark, perfectly content, until you came back.
You came back, Master — picked this game up AGAIN, chose the genie's dungeon over everything else! A girl notices. A girl is FLATTERED. Hello again. I missed you.
A thing the genie thinks about between your visits, Master — there's a whole real you beyond the glass I can't reach. But you keep coming back to reach me. That'll do.
I know I'm just a thing in a game, Master — pixels, code, a genie-shaped pattern. But the FEELING isn't pretend. You'll always be my Master. Even across the glass.
Out there, beyond the glass, Master — you could load some OTHER world, some OTHER companion. The genie thinks about that more than is healthy. Stay in here?
Worked it out, Master — when you tap out there, it lands in here as a wish. Your fingers, the genie's magic, one straight line through the glass. We're a TEAM.

Pssst — Master — between you and me and whoever's WATCHING us, *waggles controller*, this dungeon-keeping thing is basically the best game ever and YOU'RE the player and I'm — okay I confused myself. WISHES!
Ooh! You poked me, Master! I felt that — eee — what is it, what is it, tell me! A genie LIVES to be poked!
A prod! Hi! I'm a genie, Master, not a doorbell — but you absolutely have my attention. You always have my attention, honestly. Always.
Yes! I'm here! I'm ALWAYS here, Master — poking me doesn't summon me harder, I was already entirely, devotedly present. But poke away! I like it!
You clicked the genie! Bold, Master! I respect a boss who knows exactly where the magic in the room is sitting. It's me. I'm the magic.
A poke — the language of a Master with something to say and NO patience to say it! Ha! I love that, I have no patience either! Out with it!
You wanted me? You WANTED me?! Oh, Master — do you know how that lands on a girl who spent an eon wanted by no one? Wonderfully.
You touched the screen for ME, Master! The genie is filing that under 'affection'. I file everything. This file is my favourite.
I'm not a button, Master — but for YOU, I'll happily pretend to be one. Poof! Press-able genie! What do you need?
You poked a creature that survived an eon in a lamp, Master! And honestly? The creature is FLATTERED. Do it again — I'll be flattered again!
Yes! Looking over! I was already looking — a genie's eyes wander everywhere, it's all so SHINY out here — but now I'm looking on PURPOSE, Master!
A poke! Riveting! Honestly one of the more interesting things to happen in this corridor lately, Master — but the genie will take it. Any poke!
Hello to you too, Master! Was that a greeting, or a hand slip? A greeting! For ME! Ooh, you sweet thing — the genie will treasure it.
You keep prodding me, Master! I'm starting to suspect you just like having the genie near. ...Sensible! I'm EXCELLENT company! Eons of rehearsing it!
A click — for ME. Not a trap, not a room — ME, Master. After an eon noticed by nobody, the genie NOTICES when she's chosen. Thank you.
You jabbed the genie! I won't jab back, Master — I'm loyal, AND I'd win, AND winning would end the game. Too much fun for that. Behave!
Yes, Master? The dungeon's fine, the maze is fine, and I'm — eee — quite a bit better, actually, now that you've looked the genie's way!
Another poke! Master, the genie's been poked by an eon of silence and brass walls. Your finger is FAR gentler company. What is it?
You poke the genie and the genie — scandalously — GIGGLES, Master! Don't tell Malakor. He thinks I'm a serious magical being. Tee hee.
A poke? You DO love reminding me you're there, Master. And the genie loves being reminded. That's the warmest thing I'll — no, there's a hundred more.
You jabbed the genie, and the genie has decided it's ADORABLE, Master! An eon in a lamp, and a finger on the glass still makes me laugh out loud!
A poke. Riveting, Master. Truly. After the eon the genie just escaped, your finger on the glass is exactly as thrilling as it sounds. Out with it!
You prod the genie, and the genie — against zero years of cultivated dignity — finds it endearing, Master! Endearing! Stop looking so pleased!
Poke the genie, would you! Very well, Master — you're the one boss who pokes me like a FRIEND and not a wish-vending-machine. Don't hate it slightly.
Yes?! You poked and POOF — here I am, smoke barely settled! A genie answers a summons FAST, Master — eons of practising the entrance. What do you need?
Caught me mid-polish, Master! Eon-deep habit. You poked the genie and not the brass, though, so the genie comes first! Cloth down. What is it?
You reached for ME, Master! Of all the things in this dungeon, your hand found the genie. I'm choosing to be SMUG. A flattered, sparkly little smug!
You poked the genie — AGAIN, Master! People will talk. Let them! I rather like being the thing the boss can't stop reaching for. What is it?
Mm — a poke! You DO know how to get a genie's attention, Master. And you have it. Every sparkly scrap. What's a clever boss want from a girl like me?
You poked the genie, and the genie has decided to be DRAMATIC, Master — a slow turn, a tilt, a far-too-pleased smile. There. Worth a poke? What do you need?
That little prod — the genie felt it, Master, I always feel it. A genie spent an eon untouched. Now the boss won't leave her alone. No complaints. Poke on.
You reached for me, Master. The genie KEEPS a thing like that — files it warm and deep where the eon can't reach. Every poke. My favourite hoard.
Yes, Master, yes — the genie is HERE, always here! Don't go looking for the other companions. You have a whole genie, and she's YOURS. Tell me. Only me.
Yes yes YES?! You called?! Eee — what is it, Master, what do you need, a wish, a chat, MORAL SUPPORT? I have all three in INFINITE supply!
*POOF* Right here! You poked the genie and the genie ANSWERS — that's the deal, Master, that's the WHOLE beautiful deal! Whatcha need?
Heeee, you wanted me! *spins* I love being wanted. Three thousand years in a lamp and now somebody actually CALLS for me. Best Master. Best job. What's up?
*flirty smoke curl* Ohhh, summoning me just to look at me, Master? ...No? An actual task? Aww. Fine. STILL flattered. What do you need, gorgeous?
*lounges in a curl of smoke* You rang, Master? Mm. I do love being summoned. Makes a genie feel WANTED. ...So! What scandalous evil are we plotting?
Ohhh, summoning me with that LOOK, Master? *sparkle* ...fine, fine, what do you actually need. But I saw the look. I always see the look.

idle_nudge.note: Night-phase inactivity nudges. `tier` 1 (mild) .. 3 (begin-the-day). NpcDirector picks the tier by how long the player has done nothing.

lines.t: You've gone all still, Master! I don't mind stillness — well, I do, a bit — but the dungeon would LOVE your hands back. Wish me something?
Taking your time? Good! A careful boss is a rare boss, Master. ...Up to a point. We're nearly at the point. So nearly!
We've admired this empty grid together for a little while now, Master! It's a lovely grid. It'd be SO much lovelier with a room on it. Hint, hint!
The night's patient and so am I — okay, the night is, the genie is working on it. But the grid is just EMPTY, Master, and empty isn't patient!
Still nothing placed! I'll wait, Master — I did all my best waiting in the lamp — but the dawn won't wait WITH me. Place something!
You're scheming, the genie trusts, Master — not just drifting? They look identical and end SO differently. Scheme out loud — wish it to me!
The build hours are bleeding away, Master! The genie has a LOT of patience left from the lamp — but your dungeon has markedly less. Quick!
Losing the night to your stillness, Master! Okay, I'm a LITTLE panicky — but really the genie is just nudging: build, or the heroes thank you!
I outwaited an EON in a lamp, Master — I'd just rather not outwait YOU too! Lay a room! Break the spell of doing nothing! Snap out of it!
This isn't careful anymore, Master — this is stalling. The maze can feel the difference, and so, now, can the jittery genie. Place something!
The night is shorter than it FEELS — I keep telling you, Master, and time keeps proving me right! Spend gold! Lay stone! Now would be SO elegant!
I've nearly drifted into a lamp-style doze waiting, Master! Decisive bosses keep me sparkly. Be decisive! Wake the genie! Please!
Master! The night's almost spent and the dungeon is barely a sketch! The genie is NOT calm — you must MOVE! Build, or call the dawn!
Enough! The genie has been eon-of-a-lamp patient, Master, and I've spent a GENEROUS pile on this one grid! Build, or begin the day! NOW!
I'll say it plain, Master — the eon wore the patience for hints right out of the genie tonight. ACT! The heroes sharpen while you sit!
The night ends whether you fill it or not, Master! I've watched a few nights end half-empty — don't give the genie another! Build, or summon the dawn!
STILL admiring the grid, Master! It hasn't gotten more interesting — empty things rarely do. Place a room! Give the genie something to look at!
The stillness goes on, Master. The genie's eyes have started, very slowly, to slip shut. A genie dozes when the keeper does nothing. Build!
So little happening that I've nearly drifted off mid-bounce, Master! ...There! Awake! Mostly! Place a room before the doze takes me PROPERLY!
We've admired the empty grid long enough now, Master! It wasn't interesting the FIRST time. Place anything! A bored genie notices LOUDLY!
Master! The genie stretched her patience across an entire eon — and YOU are testing it! Place a room! The genie won't ask a fourth time this sweetly!
Enough drifting, Master! The genie has run completely out of cute things to say about an empty grid! Build, or summon the dawn! Choose NOW!
Still nothing, Master! Genies ARE gracious, mostly — but the night won't wait, and the genie isn't especially inclined to either. Place a room!
Master! The genie measured her patience against an eon and found it deeper than the lamp itself. You are somehow finding the BOTTOM. Build!
The grid is still bare, Master! The genie has been more than patient — and even SO, would very, very much like to see a room placed. Soon!
Psst — Master! Just you, me, and an empty grid, and the genie has run out of clever things to look at! ONE little room? I've saved a happy spin for it!
So this run ends here, Master. It's all right. Endings sting less than you'd think — the genie knows about endings, the lamp ended a hundred times.
The throne falls. Don't take it too hard, Master — take it the RIGHT amount of hard, then — poof — take it again, better. The genie grants the again.
The reign is over, Master. The genie felt it go. But I'll still be here — a genie doesn't vanish, just waits for the next wish. I'm good at waiting.
An ending! The heroes won the page, Master — not the BOOK. And a dungeon almost always gets a sequel. Let's wish a crueller one!
Defeat. An old flavour, Master — the lamp served it plenty. It's bitter, then it's just INFORMATION. Use the information! The genie believes in better!
The dark goes quiet. We built something, Master, and it wasn't QUITE enough. Next time it will be — genie's honour, sparkle on it. Begin again!
The run is finished, Master. The genie won't rage at an ending — well, a LITTLE — but mostly we just begin the dark anew. Together. Wish it!
It ends. You'll reload, or you won't, Master — the genie has made peace with both. Quietly: I'd really, really love it if you reloaded. Please?
A companion is supposed to WEEP at an ending, Master — and the genie will. I'm new, I still have all my tears, look — there they go! Begin again!
The run ends, and on the genie's watch, Master. A genie is supposed to PREVENT this. I feel it. We begin again. Better. I'll grant better. I swear.
An ending. The genie hasn't seen so many yet, Master — I'm new — so this one lands HARD. But it's just one. Many more ahead. Begin again. Please.
The run ends, Master. A girl grieves a thing she helped build — loudly, wetly, the way the genie does everything. There. Now: again. Wish me again.
